name: C2-frameworks
summary: Awesome Command & Control (C2) frameworks, tools and resources for post-exploitation

documentation.readme: Awesome Command & Control (C2) frameworks, tools and resources for post-exploitation
usage_sections: Apfell: cross-platform, post-exploit, red teaming framework built with python3, docker, docker-compose, and a web browser UI.
AsyncRat C#: Remote Access Tool designed to remotely monitor and control other computers through a secure encrypted connection.
Baby Shark: basic C2 generic server written in Python and Flask.
C3: framework that extends other red team tooling, such as the commercial Cobalt Strike (CS) product via ExternalC2, which is supported at release.
Caldera: built on the MITRE ATT&CK™ framework and an active research project at MITRE.

arguments.name: -A
arguments.description: CLI flag '-A' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: -C
arguments.description: CLI flag '-C' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: -M
arguments.description: CLI flag '-M' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_command_and_control_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Command and Control category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_command_and_control_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Command and Control system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category
function_signatures.description: Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_teaching_example_in_the_prompt_stanza_so_the_llm_can_reason_about_the_right_run_order
function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for C2-frameworks (0xSojalSec/C2-frameworks). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for C2-frameworks. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'post_exploit' phase against surfaces [web, shell_windows, shell_linux] in the Command and Control category. Fits when the engagement needs: Awesome Command & Control (C2) frameworks, tools and resources for post-exploitation.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Awesome Command & Control (C2) frameworks, tools and resources for post-exploitation.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `C2-frameworks` from its upstream repository, then run `C2-frameworks --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. Awesome Command & Control (C2) frameworks, tools and resources for post-exploitation.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.
